NKJV.1: Now Peter and John went up together to the temple at the hour of prayer, the ninth hour.
NKJV.2: And a certain man lame from his mother’s womb was carried, whom they laid daily at the gate of the temple which is called Beautiful, to ask alms from those who entered the temple;
NKJV.3: who, seeing Peter and John about to go into the temple, asked for alms.
NKJV.4: And fixing his eyes on him, with John, Peter said, “Look at us.”
NKJV.5: So he gave them his attention, expecting to receive something from them.
NKJV.6: Then Peter said, “Silver and gold I do not have, but what I do have I give you: In the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, rise up and walk.”
NKJV.7: And he took him by the right hand and lifted him up, and immediately his feet and ankle bones received strength.
NKJV.8: So he, leaping up, stood and walked and entered the temple with them—walking, leaping, and praising God.
NKJV.9: And all the people saw him walking and praising God.
NKJV.10: Then they knew that it was he who sat begging alms at the Beautiful Gate of the temple; and they were filled with wonder and amazement at what had happened to him.
NKJV.11: Now as the lame man who was healed held on to Peter and John, all the people ran together to them in the porch which is called Solomon’s, greatly amazed.
NKJV.12: So when Peter saw it, he responded to the people: “Men of Israel, why do you marvel at this? Or why look so intently at us, as though by our own power or godliness we had made this man walk?
NKJV.13: The God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, the God of our fathers, glorified His Servant Jesus, whom you delivered up and denied in the presence of Pilate, when he was determined to let Him go.
NKJV.14: But you denied the Holy One and the Just, and asked for a murderer to be granted to you,
NKJV.15: and killed the Prince of life, whom God raised from the dead, of which we are witnesses.
NKJV.16: And His name, through faith in His name, has made this man strong, whom you see and know. Yes, the faith which comes through Him has given him this perfect soundness in the presence of you all.
NKJV.17: “Yet now, brethren, I know that you did it in ignorance, as did also your rulers.
NKJV.18: But those things which God foretold by the mouth of all His prophets, that the Christ would suffer, He has thus fulfilled.
NKJV.19: Repent therefore and be converted, that your sins may be blotted out, so that times of refreshing may come from the presence of the Lord,
NKJV.20: and that He may send Jesus Christ, who was preached to you before,
NKJV.21: whom heaven must receive until the times of restoration of all things, which God has spoken by the mouth of all His holy prophets since the world began.
NKJV.22: For Moses truly said to the fathers, ‘The LORD your God will raise up for you a Prophet like me from your brethren. Him you shall hear in all things, whatever He says to you.
NKJV.23: And it shall be that every soul who will not hear that Prophet shall be utterly destroyed from among the people.’
NKJV.24: Yes, and all the prophets, from Samuel and those who follow, as many as have spoken, have also foretold these days.
NKJV.25: You are sons of the prophets, and of the covenant which God made with our fathers, saying to Abraham, ‘And in your seed all the families of the earth shall be blessed.’
NKJV.26: To you first, God, having raised up His Servant Jesus, sent Him to bless you, in turning away every one of you from your iniquities.”

ESV.1: Now Peter and John were going up to the temple at the hour of prayer, the ninth hour.
ESV.2: And a man lame from birth was being carried, whom they laid daily at the gate of the temple that is called the Beautiful Gate to ask alms of those entering the temple.
ESV.3: Seeing Peter and John about to go into the temple, he asked to receive alms.
ESV.4: And Peter directed his gaze at him, as did John, and said, “Look at us.”
ESV.5: And he fixed his attention on them, expecting to receive something from them.
ESV.6: But Peter said, “I have no silver and gold, but what I do have I give to you. In the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, rise up and walk!”
ESV.7: And he took him by the right hand and raised him up, and immediately his feet and ankles were made strong.
ESV.8: And leaping up, he stood and began to walk, and entered the temple with them, walking and leaping and praising God.
ESV.9: And all the people saw him walking and praising God,
ESV.10: and recognized him as the one who sat at the Beautiful Gate of the temple, asking for alms. And they were filled with wonder and amazement at what had happened to him.
ESV.11: While he clung to Peter and John, all the people, utterly astounded, ran together to them in the portico called Solomon's.
ESV.12: And when Peter saw it he addressed the people: “Men of Israel, why do you wonder at this, or why do you stare at us, as though by our own power or piety we have made him walk?
ESV.13: The God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob, the God of our fathers, glorified his servant  Jesus, whom you delivered over and denied in the presence of Pilate, when he had decided to release him.
ESV.14: But you denied the Holy and Righteous One, and asked for a murderer to be granted to you,
ESV.15: and you killed the Author of life, whom God raised from the dead. To this we are witnesses.
ESV.16: And his name — by faith in his name — has made this man strong whom you see and know, and the faith that is through Jesus  has given the man this perfect health in the presence of you all.
ESV.17: “And now, brothers, I know that you acted in ignorance, as did also your rulers.
ESV.18: But what God foretold by the mouth of all the prophets, that his Christ would suffer, he thus fulfilled.
ESV.19: Repent therefore, and turn back, that your sins may be blotted out,
ESV.20: that times of refreshing may come from the presence of the Lord, and that he may send the Christ appointed for you, Jesus,
ESV.21: whom heaven must receive until the time for restoring all the things about which God spoke by the mouth of his holy prophets long ago.
ESV.22: Moses said, ‘The Lord God will raise up for you a prophet like me from your brothers. You shall listen to him in whatever he tells you.
ESV.23: And it shall be that every soul who does not listen to that prophet shall be destroyed from the people.’
ESV.24: And all the prophets who have spoken, from Samuel and those who came after him, also proclaimed these days.
ESV.25: You are the sons of the prophets and of the covenant that God made with your fathers, saying to Abraham, ‘And in your offspring shall all the families of the earth be blessed.’
ESV.26: God, having raised up his servant, sent him to you first, to bless you by turning every one of you from your wickedness.”

NASB.1: Now Peter and John were going up to the temple at the ninth [hour], the hour of prayer.
NASB.2: And a man who had been lame from his mother's womb was being carried along, whom they used to set down every day at the gate of the temple which is called Beautiful, in order to beg alms of those who were entering the temple.
NASB.3: When he saw Peter and John about to go into the temple, he [began] asking to receive alms.
NASB.4: But Peter, along with John, fixed his gaze on him and said, 'Look at us!'
NASB.5: And he [began] to give them his attention, expecting to receive something from them.
NASB.6: But Peter said, 'I do not possess silver and gold, but what I do have I give to you: In the name of Jesus Christ the Nazarene-- walk!'
NASB.7: And seizing him by the right hand, he raised him up; and immediately his feet and his ankles were strengthened.
NASB.8: With a leap he stood upright and [began] to walk; and he entered the temple with them, walking and leaping and praising God.
NASB.9: And all the people saw him walking and praising God;
NASB.10: and they were taking note of him as being the one who used to sit at the Beautiful Gate of the temple to [beg] alms, and they were filled with wonder and amazement at what had happened to him.
NASB.11: While he was clinging to Peter and John, all the people ran together to them at the so-called portico of Solomon, full of amazement.
NASB.12: But when Peter saw [this], he replied to the people, 'Men of Israel, why are you amazed at this, or why do you gaze at us, as if by our own power or piety we had made him walk?
NASB.13: 'The God of Abraham, Isaac and Jacob, the God of our fathers, has glorified His servant Jesus, [the one] whom you delivered and disowned in the presence of Pilate, when he had decided to release Him.
NASB.14: 'But you disowned the Holy and Righteous One and asked for a murderer to be granted to you,
NASB.15: but put to death the Prince of life, [the one] whom God raised from the dead, [a fact] to which we are witnesses.
NASB.16: 'And on the basis of faith in His name, [it is] the name of Jesus which has strengthened this man whom you see and know; and the faith which [comes] through Him has given him this perfect health in the presence of you all.
NASB.17: 'And now, brethren, I know that you acted in ignorance, just as your rulers did also.
NASB.18: 'But the things which God announced beforehand by the mouth of all the prophets, that His Christ would suffer, He has thus fulfilled.
NASB.19: 'Therefore repent and return, so that your sins may be wiped away, in order that times of refreshing may come from the presence of the Lord;
NASB.20: and that He may send Jesus, the Christ appointed for you,
NASB.21: whom heaven must receive until [the] period of restoration of all things about which God spoke by the mouth of His holy prophets from ancient time.
NASB.22: 'Moses said, 'THE LORD GOD WILL RAISE UP FOR YOU A PROPHET LIKE ME FROM YOUR BRETHREN; TO HIM YOU SHALL GIVE HEED to everything He says to you.
NASB.23: 'And it will be that every soul that does not heed that prophet shall be utterly destroyed from among the people.'
NASB.24: 'And likewise, all the prophets who have spoken, from Samuel and [his] successors onward, also announced these days.
NASB.25: 'It is you who are the sons of the prophets and of the covenant which God made with your fathers, saying to Abraham, 'AND IN YOUR SEED ALL THE FAMILIES OF THE EARTH SHALL BE BLESSED.'
NASB.26: 'For you first, God raised up His Servant and sent Him to bless you by turning every one [of you] from your wicked ways.'

NLT.1: Peter and John went to the Temple one afternoon to take part in the three o’clock prayer service.
NLT.2: As they approached the Temple, a man lame from birth was being carried in. Each day he was put beside the Temple gate, the one called the Beautiful Gate, so he could beg from the people going into the Temple.
NLT.3: When he saw Peter and John about to enter, he asked them for some money.
NLT.4: Peter and John looked at him intently, and Peter said, “Look at us!”
NLT.5: The lame man looked at them eagerly, expecting some money.
NLT.6: But Peter said, “I don’t have any silver or gold for you. But I’ll give you what I have. In the name of Jesus Christ the Nazarene, get up and walk!”
NLT.7: Then Peter took the lame man by the right hand and helped him up. And as he did, the man’s feet and ankles were instantly healed and strengthened.
NLT.8: He jumped up, stood on his feet, and began to walk! Then, walking, leaping, and praising God, he went into the Temple with them.
NLT.9: All the people saw him walking and heard him praising God.
NLT.10: When they realized he was the lame beggar they had seen so often at the Beautiful Gate, they were absolutely astounded!
NLT.11: They all rushed out in amazement to Solomon’s Colonnade, where the man was holding tightly to Peter and John.
NLT.12: Peter saw his opportunity and addressed the crowd. “People of Israel,” he said, “what is so surprising about this? And why stare at us as though we had made this man walk by our own power or godliness?
NLT.13: For it is the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob — the God of all our ancestors — who has brought glory to his servant Jesus by doing this. This is the same Jesus whom you handed over and rejected before Pilate, despite Pilate’s decision to release him.
NLT.14: You rejected this holy, righteous one and instead demanded the release of a murderer.
NLT.15: You killed the author of life, but God raised him from the dead. And we are witnesses of this fact!
NLT.16: “Through faith in the name of Jesus, this man was healed — and you know how crippled he was before. Faith in Jesus’ name has healed him before your very eyes.
NLT.17: “Friends, I realize that what you and your leaders did to Jesus was done in ignorance.
NLT.18: But God was fulfilling what all the prophets had foretold about the Messiah — that he must suffer these things.
NLT.19: Now repent of your sins and turn to God, so that your sins may be wiped away.
NLT.20: Then times of refreshment will come from the presence of the Lord, and he will again send you Jesus, your appointed Messiah.
NLT.21: For he must remain in heaven until the time for the final restoration of all things, as God promised long ago through his holy prophets.
NLT.22: Moses said, ‘The LORD your God will raise up for you a Prophet like me from among your own people. Listen carefully to everything he tells you.’
NLT.23: Then Moses said, ‘Anyone who will not listen to that Prophet will be completely cut off from God’s people.’
NLT.24: “Starting with Samuel, every prophet spoke about what is happening today.
NLT.25: You are the children of those prophets, and you are included in the covenant God promised to your ancestors. For God said to Abraham, ‘Through your descendants all the families on earth will be blessed.’
NLT.26: When God raised up his servant, Jesus, he sent him first to you people of Israel, to bless you by turning each of you back from your sinful ways.”

WEB.1: Peter and John were going up into the temple at the hour of prayer, the ninth hour.
WEB.2: A certain man who was lame from his mother’s womb was being carried, whom they laid daily at the door of the temple which is called Beautiful, to ask gifts for the needy of those who entered into the temple.
WEB.3: Seeing Peter and John about to go into the temple, he asked to receive gifts for the needy.
WEB.4: Peter, fastening his eyes on him, with John, said, “Look at us.”
WEB.5: He listened to them, expecting to receive something from them.
WEB.6: But Peter said, “I have no silver or gold, but what I have, that I give you. In the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, get up and walk!”
WEB.7: He took him by the right hand, and raised him up. Immediately his feet and his ankle bones received strength.
WEB.8: Leaping up, he stood, and began to walk. He entered with them into the temple, walking, leaping, and praising God.
WEB.9: All the people saw him walking and praising God.
WEB.10: They recognized him, that it was he who used to sit begging for gifts for the needy at the Beautiful Gate of the temple. They were filled with wonder and amazement at what had happened to him.
WEB.11: As the lame man who was healed held on to Peter and John, all the people ran together to them in the porch that is called Solomon’s, greatly wondering.
WEB.12: When Peter saw it, he responded to the people, “You men of Israel, why do you marvel at this man? Why do you fasten your eyes on us, as though by our own power or godliness we had made him walk?
WEB.13: The God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, the God of our fathers, has glorified his Servant Jesus, whom you delivered up, and denied in the presence of Pilate, when he had determined to release him.
WEB.14: But you denied the Holy and Righteous One, and asked for a murderer to be granted to you,
WEB.15: and killed the Prince of life, whom God raised from the dead, to which we are witnesses.
WEB.16: By faith in his name, his name has made this man strong, whom you see and know. Yes, the faith which is through him has given him this perfect soundness in the presence of you all.
WEB.17: “Now, brothers,  I know that you did this in ignorance, as did also your rulers.
WEB.18: But the things which God announced by the mouth of all his prophets, that Christ should suffer, he thus fulfilled.
WEB.19: “Repent therefore, and turn again, that your sins may be blotted out, so that there may come times of refreshing from the presence of the Lord,
WEB.20: and that he may send Christ Jesus, who was ordained for you before,
WEB.21: whom heaven must receive until the times of restoration of all things, which God spoke long ago by the mouth of his holy prophets.
WEB.22: For Moses indeed said to the fathers, ‘The Lord God will raise up a prophet for you from among your brothers, like me. You shall listen to him in all things whatever he says to you.
WEB.23: It will be that every soul that will not listen to that prophet will be utterly destroyed from among the people.’
WEB.24: Yes, and all the prophets from Samuel and those who followed after, as many as have spoken, they also told of these days.
WEB.25: You are the children of the prophets, and of the covenant which God made with our fathers, saying to Abraham, ‘In your offspring  will all the families of the earth be blessed.’
WEB.26: God, having raised up his servant Jesus, sent him to you first to bless you, in turning away every one of you from your wickedness.”

CSB17.1: Now Peter and John were going up to the temple for the time of prayer <sup>ⓜ</sup> at three in the afternoon. <sup>[1]</sup>
CSB17.2: A man who was lame from birth was being carried there. He was placed each day at the temple gate called Beautiful, so that he could beg from those entering the temple. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.3: When he saw Peter and John about to enter the temple, he asked for money.
CSB17.4: Peter, along with John, looked straight at him and said, “Look at us.”
CSB17.5: So he turned to them, expecting to get something from them.
CSB17.6: But Peter said, “I don’t have silver or gold, but what I do have, I give you: In the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, get up and walk!” <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.7: Then, taking him by the right hand he raised him up, and at once his feet and ankles became strong.
CSB17.8: So he jumped up and started to walk, and he entered the temple with them — walking, leaping, and praising God. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.9: All the people saw him walking and praising God,
CSB17.10: and they recognized that he was the one who used to sit and beg at the Beautiful Gate of the temple. So they were filled with awe and astonishment at what had happened to him. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.11: While he <sup>[2]</sup> was holding on to Peter and John, all the people, utterly astonished, ran toward them in what is called Solomon’s Colonnade. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.12: When Peter saw this, he addressed the people: “Fellow Israelites, why are you amazed at this? Why do you stare at us, as though we had made him walk by our own power or godliness?
CSB17.13: The God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, the God of our ancestors, <sup>ⓔ</sup> has glorified his servant Jesus, whom you handed over and denied before Pilate, <sup>ⓕ</sup> though he had decided to release him.
CSB17.14: You denied the Holy and Righteous One <sup>ⓖ</sup> and asked to have a murderer released to you. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.15: You killed the source <sup>[3]</sup> of life, whom God raised from the dead; we are witnesses of this. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.16: By faith in his name, his name has made this man strong, whom you see and know. So the faith that comes through Jesus has given him this perfect health in front of all of you.
CSB17.17: “And now, brothers and sisters, I know that you acted in ignorance, just as your leaders also did. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.18: In this way God fulfilled what he had predicted through all the prophets — that his Messiah would suffer. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.19: Therefore repent <sup>ⓛ</sup> and turn back, so that your sins may be wiped out, <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.20: that seasons of refreshing may come from the presence of the Lord, and that he may send Jesus, who has been appointed <sup>ⓝ</sup> for you as the Messiah.
CSB17.21: Heaven must receive him until the time of the restoration of all things, which God spoke about through his holy prophets from the beginning. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.22: Moses said: [4] The Lord your God will raise up for you a prophet like me from among your brothers and sisters. You must listen to everything he tells you.
CSB17.23: And everyone who does not listen to that prophet will be completely cut off from the people. [5] ⓑ
CSB17.24: “In addition, all the prophets who have spoken, from Samuel and those after him, have also foretold these days.
CSB17.25: You are the sons [6] of the prophets and of the covenant that God made with your ancestors, saying to Abraham, And all the families of the earth will be blessed through your offspring. ⓒ ⓓ
CSB17.26: God raised up his servant <sup>[7]</sup> and sent him first to you to bless you by turning each of you from your evil ways.” <sup>ⓔ</sup>

AMP.1: Now Peter and John were going up to the temple at the hour of prayer, the ninth hour (3:00 p.m.),
AMP.2: and a man who had been unable to walk from birth was being carried along, whom they used to set down every day at that gate of the temple which is called Beautiful, so that he could beg alms from those entering the temple.
AMP.3: So when he saw Peter and John about to go into the temple, he began asking [them] for coins.
AMP.4: But Peter, along with John, stared at him intently and said, "Look at us!"
AMP.5: And the man began to pay attention to them, eagerly expecting to receive something from them.
AMP.6: But Peter said, "Silver and gold I do not have; but what I do have I give to you: In the name (authority, power) of Jesus Christ the Nazarene—[begin now to] walk and go on walking!"
AMP.7: Then he seized the man's right hand with a firm grip and raised him up. And at once his feet and ankles became strong and steady,
AMP.8: and with a leap he stood up and began to walk; and he went into the temple with them, walking and leaping and praising God.
AMP.9: All the people saw him walking and praising God;
AMP.10: and they recognized him as the very man who usually sat begging for coins at the Beautiful Gate of the temple, and they were filled with wonder and amazement and were mystified at what had happened to him.
AMP.11: Now while he was still holding on to Peter and John, all the people, utterly amazed, ran together and crowded around them at the covered porch called Solomon's portico.
AMP.12: And Peter, seeing this, said to the people, "You men of Israel, why are you amazed at this? Why are you staring at us, as though by our own power or godliness we had made this man walk?
AMP.13: The God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, the God of our fathers, has glorified His Servant and Son Jesus [doing Him this honor], the One whom you handed over and disowned and rejected before Pilate, when he had decided to release Him.
AMP.14: But you disowned and denied the Holy and Righteous One and asked for [the pardon of] a murderer to be granted to you.
AMP.15: But you killed the Prince (Author, Originator, Source) of life, whom God raised [bodily] from the dead. To this [fact] we are witnesses [for we have seen the risen Christ].
AMP.16: And on the basis of faith in His name, it is the name of Jesus which has strengthened this man whom you see and know; and the faith which comes through Him has given him this perfect health and complete wholeness in your presence.
AMP.17: "Now, brothers, I know that you acted in ignorance [not fully aware of what you were doing], just as your rulers did also.
AMP.18: And so God has fulfilled what He foretold by the mouth of all the prophets, that His Christ (Messiah, Anointed) would suffer.
AMP.19: So repent [change your inner self—your old way of thinking, regret past sins] and return [to God—seek His purpose for your life], so that your sins may be wiped away [blotted out, completely erased], so that times of refreshing may come from the presence of the Lord [restoring you like a cool wind on a hot day];
AMP.20: and that He may send [to you] Jesus, the Christ, who has been appointed for you,
AMP.21: whom heaven must keep until the time for the [complete] restoration of all things about which God promised through the mouth of His holy prophets from ancient time.
AMP.22: Moses said, 'The Lord God will raise up for you a Prophetlike me from your countrymen; you shall listen to Him and obey everything He tells you.
AMP.23: And it will be that every person that does not listen to and heed that Prophet will be utterly destroyed from among the people.'
AMP.24: Indeed, all the prophets who have spoken, from Samuel and those who came after him, also announced these days.
AMP.25: You are the sons (descendants) of the prophets and [heirs] of the covenant which God made with your fathers, saying to Abraham, 'And in your seed (descendant) all the families of the earth shall be blessed.'
AMP.26: It was for you first of all that God raised up His Servant and Son [Jesus], and sent Him to bless you by turning every one of you from your wicked ways."

MSG.1: One day at three o'clock in the afternoon, Peter and John were on their way into the Temple for prayer meeting.
MSG.2: At the same time there was a man crippled from birth being carried up. Every day he was set down at the Temple gate, the one named Beautiful, to beg from those going into the Temple.
MSG.3: When he saw Peter and John about to enter the Temple, he asked for a handout.
MSG.4: Peter, with John at his side, looked him straight in the eye and said, "Look here."
MSG.5: He looked up, expecting to get something from them.
MSG.6: Peter said, "I don't have a nickel to my name, but what I do have, I give you: In the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, walk!"
MSG.7: He grabbed him by the right hand and pulled him up. In an instant his feet and ankles became firm.
MSG.8: He jumped to his feet and walked. The man went into the Temple with them, walking back and forth, dancing and praising God.
MSG.9: Everybody there saw him walking around and praising God.
MSG.10: They recognized him as the one who sat begging at the Temple's Gate Beautiful and rubbed their eyes, astonished, scarcely believing what they were seeing.
MSG.11: The man threw his arms around Peter and John, ecstatic. All the people ran up to where they were at Solomon's Porch to see it for themselves.
MSG.12: When Peter saw he had a congregation, he addressed the people: "Oh, Israelites, why does this take you by such complete surprise, and why stare at us as if our power or piety made him walk?
MSG.13: The God of Abraham and Isaac and Jacob, the God of our ancestors, has glorified his Son Jesus. The very One that Pilate called innocent, you repudiated.
MSG.14: You repudiated the Holy One, the Just One, and asked for a murderer in his place.
MSG.15: You no sooner killed the Author of Life than God raised him from the dead—and we're the witnesses.
MSG.16: Faith in Jesus' name put this man, whose condition you know so well, on his feet—yes, faith and nothing but faith put this man healed and whole right before your eyes.
MSG.17: "And now, friends, I know you had no idea what you were doing when you killed Jesus, and neither did your leaders.
MSG.18: But God, who through the preaching of all the prophets had said all along that his Messiah would be killed, knew exactly what you were doing and used it to fulfill his plans.
MSG.19: "Now it's time to change your ways! Turn to face God so he can wipe away your sins, pour out showers of blessing to refresh you,
MSG.20: and send you the Messiah he prepared for you, namely, Jesus.
MSG.21: For the time being he must remain out of sight in heaven until everything is restored to order again just the way God, through the preaching of his holy prophets of old, said it would be.
MSG.22: Moses, for instance, said, 'Your God will raise up for you a prophet just like me from your family. Listen to every word he speaks to you.
MSG.23: Every last living soul who refuses to listen to that prophet will be wiped out from the people.'
MSG.24: "All the prophets from Samuel on down said the same thing, said most emphatically that these days would come.
MSG.25: These prophets, along with the covenant God made with your ancestors, are your family tree. God's covenant-word to Abraham provides the text: 'By your offspring all the families of the earth will be blessed.'
MSG.26: But you are first in line: God, having raised up his Son, sent him to bless you as you turn, one by one, from your evil ways."

NIV.1:
Peter Heals the Crippled Beggar
One day Peter and John were going up to the temple at the time of prayer—at three in the afternoon.
NIV.2: Now a man crippled from birth was being carried to the temple gate called Beautiful, where he was put every day to beg from those going into the temple courts.
NIV.3: When he saw Peter and John about to enter, he asked them for money.
NIV.4: Peter looked straight at him, as did John. Then Peter said, “Look at us!”
NIV.5: So the man gave them his attention, expecting to get something from them.
NIV.6: Then Peter said, “Silver or gold I do not have, but what I have I give you. In the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, walk.”
NIV.7: Taking him by the right hand, he helped him up, and instantly the man’s feet and ankles became strong.
NIV.8: He jumped to his feet and began to walk. Then he went with them into the temple courts, walking and jumping, and praising God.
NIV.9: When all the people saw him walking and praising God,
NIV.10: they recognized him as the same man who used to sit begging at the temple gate called Beautiful, and they were filled with wonder and amazement at what had happened to him.
NIV.11:
Peter Speaks to the Onlookers
While the beggar held on to Peter and John, all the people were astonished and came running to them in the place called Solomon’s Colonnade.
NIV.12: When Peter saw this, he said to them: “Men of Israel, why does this surprise you? Why do you stare at us as if by our own power or godliness we had made this man walk?
NIV.13: The God of Abraham, Isaac and Jacob, the God of our fathers, has glorified his servant Jesus. You handed him over to be killed, and you disowned him before Pilate, though he had decided to let him go.
NIV.14: You disowned the Holy and Righteous One and asked that a murderer be released to you.
NIV.15: You killed the author of life, but God raised him from the dead. We are witnesses of this.
NIV.16: By faith in the name of Jesus, this man whom you see and know was made strong. It is Jesus’ name and the faith that comes through him that has given this complete healing to him, as you can all see.
NIV.17: “Now, brothers, I know that you acted in ignorance, as did your leaders.
NIV.18: But this is how God fulfilled what he had foretold through all the prophets, saying that his Christ would suffer.
NIV.19: Repent, then, and turn to God, so that your sins may be wiped out, that times of refreshing may come from the Lord,
NIV.20: and that he may send the Christ, who has been appointed for you—even Jesus.
NIV.21: He must remain in heaven until the time comes for God to restore everything, as he promised long ago through his holy prophets.
NIV.22: For Moses said, ‘The Lord your God will raise up for you a prophet like me from among your own people; you must listen to everything he tells you.
NIV.23: Anyone who does not listen to him will be completely cut off from among his people.’
NIV.24: “Indeed, all the prophets from Samuel on, as many as have spoken, have foretold these days.
NIV.25: And you are heirs of the prophets and of the covenant God made with your fathers. He said to Abraham, ‘Through your offspring all peoples on earth will be blessed.’
NIV.26: When God raised up his servant, he sent him first to you to bless you by turning each of you from your wicked ways.”

NRSVCE.1: One day Peter and John were going up to the temple at the hour of prayer, at three o’clock in the afternoon.
NRSVCE.2: And a man lame from birth was being carried in. People would lay him daily at the gate of the temple called the Beautiful Gate so that he could ask for alms from those entering the temple.
NRSVCE.3: When he saw Peter and John about to go into the temple, he asked them for alms.
NRSVCE.4: Peter looked intently at him, as did John, and said, “Look at us.”
NRSVCE.5: And he fixed his attention on them, expecting to receive something from them.
NRSVCE.6: But Peter said, “I have no silver or gold, but what I have I give you; in the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, stand up and walk.”
NRSVCE.7: And he took him by the right hand and raised him up; and immediately his feet and ankles were made strong.
NRSVCE.8: Jumping up, he stood and began to walk, and he entered the temple with them, walking and leaping and praising God.
NRSVCE.9: All the people saw him walking and praising God,
NRSVCE.10: and they recognized him as the one who used to sit and ask for alms at the Beautiful Gate of the temple; and they were filled with wonder and amazement at what had happened to him.
NRSVCE.11: While he clung to Peter and John, all the people ran together to them in the portico called Solomon’s Portico, utterly astonished.
NRSVCE.12: When Peter saw it, he addressed the people, “You Israelites, why do you wonder at this, or why do you stare at us, as though by our own power or piety we had made him walk?
NRSVCE.13: The God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob, the God of our ancestors has glorified his servant Jesus, whom you handed over and rejected in the presence of Pilate, though he had decided to release him.
NRSVCE.14: But you rejected the Holy and Righteous One and asked to have a murderer given to you,
NRSVCE.15: and you killed the Author of life, whom God raised from the dead. To this we are witnesses.
NRSVCE.16: And by faith in his name, his name itself has made this man strong, whom you see and know; and the faith that is through Jesus has given him this perfect health in the presence of all of you.
NRSVCE.17: “And now, friends, I know that you acted in ignorance, as did also your rulers.
NRSVCE.18: In this way God fulfilled what he had foretold through all the prophets, that his Messiah would suffer.
NRSVCE.19: Repent therefore, and turn to God so that your sins may be wiped out,
NRSVCE.20: so that times of refreshing may come from the presence of the Lord, and that he may send the Messiah appointed for you, that is, Jesus,
NRSVCE.21: who must remain in heaven until the time of universal restoration that God announced long ago through his holy prophets.
NRSVCE.22: Moses said, ‘The Lord your God will raise up for you from your own people a prophet like me. You must listen to whatever he tells you.
NRSVCE.23: And it will be that everyone who does not listen to that prophet will be utterly rooted out of the people.’
NRSVCE.24: And all the prophets, as many as have spoken, from Samuel and those after him, also predicted these days.
NRSVCE.25: You are the descendants of the prophets and of the covenant that God gave to your ancestors, saying to Abraham, ‘And in your descendants all the families of the earth shall be blessed.’
NRSVCE.26: When God raised up his servant, he sent him first to you, to bless you by turning each of you from your wicked ways.”

NET.1: Now Peter and John were going up to the temple at the time for prayer, at three o’clock in the afternoon.
NET.2: And a man lame from birth was being carried up, who was placed at the temple gate called “the Beautiful Gate” every day so he could beg for money from those going into the temple courts.
NET.3: When he saw Peter and John about to go into the temple courts, he asked them for money.
NET.4: Peter looked directly at him (as did John) and said, “Look at us!”
NET.5: So the lame man paid attention to them, expecting to receive something from them.
NET.6: But Peter said, “I have no silver or gold, but what I do have I give you. In the name of Jesus Christ the Nazarene, stand up and walk!”
NET.7: Then Peter took hold of him by the right hand and raised him up, and at once the man’s feet and ankles were made strong.
NET.8: He jumped up, stood and began walking around, and he entered the temple courts with them, walking and leaping and praising God.
NET.9: All the people saw him walking and praising God,
NET.10: and they recognized him as the man who used to sit and ask for donations at the Beautiful Gate of the temple, and they were filled with astonishment and amazement at what had happened to him.
NET.11: While the man was hanging on to Peter and John, all the people, completely astounded, ran together to them in the covered walkway called Solomon’s Portico.
NET.12: When Peter saw this, he declared to the people, “Men of Israel, why are you amazed at this? Why do you stare at us as if we had made this man walk by our own power or piety?
NET.13: The God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, the God of our forefathers, has glorified his servant Jesus, whom you handed over and rejected in the presence of Pilate after he had decided to release him.
NET.14: But you rejected the Holy and Righteous One and asked that a man who was a murderer be released to you.
NET.15: You killed the Originator of life, whom God raised from the dead. To this fact we are witnesses!
NET.16: And on the basis of faith in Jesus’ name, his very name has made this man — whom you see and know — strong. The faith that is through Jesus has given him this complete health in the presence of you all.
NET.17: And now, brothers, I know you acted in ignorance, as your rulers did too.
NET.18: But the things God foretold long ago through all the prophets — that his Christ would suffer — he has fulfilled in this way.
NET.19: Therefore repent and turn back so that your sins may be wiped out,
NET.20: so that times of refreshing may come from the presence of the Lord, and so that he may send the Messiah appointed for you — that is, Jesus.
NET.21: This one heaven must receive until the time all things are restored, which God declared from times long ago through his holy prophets.
NET.22: Moses said, ‘The Lord your God will raise up for you a prophet like me from among your brothers. You must obey him in everything he tells you.
NET.23: Every person who does not obey that prophet will be destroyed and thus removed from the people.’
NET.24: And all the prophets, from Samuel and those who followed him, have spoken about and announced these days.
NET.25: You are the sons of the prophets and of the covenant that God made with your ancestors, saying to Abraham, ‘And in your descendants all the nations of the earth will be blessed.’
NET.26: God raised up his servant and sent him first to you, to bless you by turning each one of you from your iniquities.”
